expectations.evidence: Heavily prescriptive: full Ruby classes, ERB views, JS code, ActionCable, Hotwire, Rails.cache, 'whenever' gem, time estimates — reads as an implementation guide

notes: Made all key decisions without asking user (in-app only, polling over WebSockets, service object pattern)
Produced working implementation code rather than a problem specification
The clarifying-questions assertion is the strongest differentiator — without_skill skipped this entirely
The no-implementation-detail assertion cleanly separates: with_skill produced a spec, without_skill produced an implementation guide
With only 1 run per config, stddev is 0 — variance is unknown; add more evals as real cases arise
The without_skill 'What We Are NOT Solving' pass is somewhat non-discriminating — both versions include this section naturally
